title: UNPARSEDENTITY
text: UNPARSEDENTITY Operation activated by the SAX parser when it encounters entity declarations in a DTD that contain the NDATA keyword. Explain this code operation UNPARSEDENTITY params string Name: in string PublicID: in string SystemID: in string Notation: in endparams Implement your code here end; UNPARSEDENTITY operation Copy Parameters Name —name of the XML entity PublicID —public identifier of the XML entity SystemID —local identifier of the XML entity Notation —name of the notation declaration Description XML streams can include references to data that does not need to be processed by the parser, such as images, executables, files, or other data. Instead, they are handled by another application or DTD, which is defined by a <!NOTATION… declaration. Data that does not need to be parsed is declared in the DTD by an <!ENTITY declaration that includes the NDATA keyword, followed by a reference to a <!NOTATION… declaration. This in turn points to the URI or application responsible for holding or processing the unparsed data. When the parser encounters entity declarations containing the NDATA keyword, it activates UNPARSEDENTITY and passes the relevant details to resolve the notation reference. For example: Explain this code <!ENTITY image SYSTEM “pic.jpg” NDATA JPG> Copy This declaration causes the parser to issue the following call to UNPARSEDENTITY : Explain this code SaxCallHandler.UNPARSEDENTITY("image", "SYSTEM", "pic.jpg", "JPG") Copy The JPG after NDATA is provided in Notation , and refers to a <!NOTATION… DTD declaration.
